title: Black No More
description: Black No More — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Black No More" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: African Repatriation Sites
memes.description: The destinations proposed by pan-Africanist organizations for Black Americans to "return" to Africa, which the novel dismisses as impractical and financially motivated schemes

memes.label: Rev. Alex McPhule
memes.description: Founder of the "Christ Lovers" cult in Happy Hill, South Carolina — a charismatic, sexually opportunistic evangelist who prays for a Black man to lynch as a sign from God, and whose fabricated "Bible" and altar-side seductions satirize Southern religious hypocrisy

memes.label: American Race Problem
memes.description: The national obsession with racial classification and conflict that the novel diagnoses as a self-perpetuating industry maintained by political, economic, and social elites on both sides of the color line

memes.label: Ancestry Revelation
memes.description: Dr. Samuel Buggerie's genealogical research for the Anglo-Saxon Association reveals that over half the American population — including the Association's own leaders — possesses non-white ancestry, triggering a crisis that leads to the data being stolen

memes.label: Angelic Orders
memes.description: The women's auxiliary organizations of the Knights of Nordica, paralleling the real Klan's Women of the Ku Klux Klan

memes.label: Anglo-Saxon
memes.description: The racial-ideological construct around which the Anglo-Saxon Association of America organizes; Schuyler's satire of the pseudo-scientific claim that Anglo-Saxons represent the highest form of whiteness.

memes.label: Anglo-Saxon Association of America
memes.description: A white supremacist political organization led by Arthur Snobbcraft that passes racial integrity laws and partners with the Knights of Nordica to dominate the post-Black-No-More political landscape

memes.label: Arthur Snobbcraft
memes.description: President of the Anglo-Saxon Association of America; an F.F.V. aristocrat whose commitment to racial purity is undercut by his own "suspiciously swarthy" complexion.

memes.label: Atlanta, Georgia
memes.description: Max Disher's hometown and the headquarters of the Knights of Nordica — the novel's Southern pole, representing Jim Crow at its most rigid and the political economy of white supremacy

memes.label: Back-to-Africa Society
memes.description: Satirical version of Marcus Garvey's UNIA, an emigrationist organization led by Santop Licorice whose fundraising collapses when the Black-No-More treatment gives its constituency an alternative route out of Blackness

memes.label: Beale Street, New York
memes.description: The Manhattan intersection at 33rd Street and Fifth Avenue, which Schuyler calls "New York's Beale Street" — a reference to the famous Memphis Black entertainment district — where tough neighborhoods and racial mixing define the urban landscape

memes.label: Beauty Culturists
memes.description: The network of Black beauty professionals — parlor operators, cosmetologists, and product sellers — whose livelihoods depended on the pre-treatment market for skin-lightening and hair-straightening services

memes.label: Better White League
memes.description: A late-novel organization formed by whitened ex-Negroes to promote their interests against "original" white people, representing the ultimate ironic reversal of racial organizing

memes.label: Black Churches
memes.description: The network of Black Christian churches whose congregations dissolve as their members undergo the Black-No-More treatment and join white congregations

memes.label: Black Colleges and Universities
memes.description: The network of historically Black colleges and universities whose mission of racial uplift is rendered obsolete by the Black-No-More treatment, referenced indirectly in the novel's academia satire

memes.label: Black Leadership Conference
memes.description: The gathering of Black "race leaders" in Chapter 5 who meet to strategize against the exodus into whiteness, revealing their self-interested motives behind the rhetoric of racial solidarity

memes.label: Black No More (Novel)
memes.description: George S. Schuyler's 1931 satirical novel — a Harlem Renaissance classic that imagines a scientific procedure turning Black people white, using the premise to excoriate American racial ideology in all its forms

memes.label: Black-No-More, Incorporated
memes.description: The commercial enterprise founded by Dr. Junius Crookman, Hank Johnson, and Chuck Foster to monetize the Black-No-More treatment — a satirical stand-in for American capitalism's ability to commodify anything, including racial identity itself

memes.label: Black-No-More Offices
memes.description: The expanding business headquarters of Black-No-More, Incorporated on Edgecombe Avenue in Harlem, where the corporation's administrative and financial operations are managed

memes.label: Black-No-More Treatment
memes.description: The fictional glandular and electrical medical procedure invented by Dr. Junius Crookman that transforms Black people into white people in three days, serving as the novel's central plot device

memes.label: Blandine's Egyptienne Stain
memes.description: The skin-darkening product invented by Sari Blandine in the novel's epilogue that reverses the racial economy — where Americans once paid to become white, they now pay to become dark

memes.label: Bunny Brown
memes.description: Max Disher's best friend and fellow Harlem socialite, who follows Max through the Black-No-More treatment and provides comic relief throughout the novel

memes.label: Central Park, New York City
memes.description: The Manhattan greenspace where Max Disher walks and broods in the novel's opening chapter — the setting for his decision to undergo the Black-No-More treatment, functioning as a threshold between his Black past and his white future

memes.label: "Chapter 1"
memes.description: "Max Disher at the Honky Tonk Club on New Year's Eve, 1933."

memes.label: "Chapter 2"
memes.description: "Slowly, haltingly, Max Disher dragged his way down the hall to the elevator, supported on either side by an attendant."

memes.description: "Dr."

memes.description: "Matthew Fisher, alias Max Disher, joined the Easter Sunday crowds, twirling his malacca stick and ogling the pretty flappers who passed giggling in their Spring finery."

memes.label: "Chapter 5"
memes.description: "A huge silver monoplane glided gracefully to the surface of Mines Field in Los Angeles and came to a pretty stop after a short run."

memes.label: "Chapter 6"
memes.description: "Two important events took place on Easter Sunday, 1934."

memes.label: "Chapter 7"
memes.description: "One June morning in 1934, Grand Exalted Giraw Fisher received a report from one of his secret operatives in the town of Paradise, South Carolina, saying:"

memes.label: "Chapter 8"
memes.description: "For over two years now had Black-No-More, Incorporated, been carrying on its self-appointed task of turning Negroes into Caucasians."

memes.label: "Chapter 9"
memes.description: "“Bunny, I’ve got it all worked out,” announced Matthew, several mornings later, as he breezed into the office."

memes.description: "Hank Johnson, Chuck Foster, Dr."

memes.description: "“What’s the matter with you, Matt?” asked Bunny one morning about a month before election."

memes.label: "Chapter 12"
memes.description: "The afternoon before election Matthew and Bunny sat in the latter’s hotel suite sipping cocktails, smoking and awaiting the inevitable."

memes.description: "Toward eleven o’clock on the evening before election day, a long, low roadster swept up to the door of a stately country home near Richmond, VA, crunched to a stop, the lights were extinguished and..."

memes.label: Index of Characters
memes.description: Complete index of all characters in Black No More, dynamically generated from page metadata. Lists every person page in the wiki sorted alphabetically by label.

memes.label: Chicago
memes.description: Site of the 1936 Republican National Convention and a recurring point of reference in the novel's satirical geography — from the Artillery Café to a humorous weekly office to the Italian gunman simile

memes.label: Dr. Samuel Buggerie
memes.description: Statistician commissioned by the Anglo-Saxon Association to conduct a nationwide genealogical investigation proving the purity of white ancestry — whose research instead reveals that "over half the population has no record of its ancestry beyond five generations" and that "too many of our very first families are touched," triggering the novel's catastrophic climax when his findings are exposed

memes.label: Chromatic Hierarchy
memes.description: The internal hierarchy among African Americans based on skin tone, from light-skinned "blue veins" to dark-skinned working class, which the novel satirizes through the Black elite's ambivalent response to the treatment

memes.label: Chuck Foster
memes.description: The cautious Black real estate speculator who manages Black-No-More, Incorporated's expansion, his conservative temperament providing a foil to Hank Johnson's exuberance

memes.label: Cincinnati
memes.description: A border city hosting a Black-No-More sanitarium and the site of the novel's most violent episode — a mob attack on a Crookman hospital that kills a dozen babies and exposes their mothers as socially prominent mulattoes

memes.label: Colonel Mortimer Roberts
memes.description: Principal of the Dusky River Agricultural Institute and leader of conservative Negroes — a figure "most of whom had nothing to conserve" — who speaks at the N.S.E.L. meeting about "cordial relations existing between the two races" and insinuates alliance with Southern militant organizations to stop Black-No-More

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Colophon — production credits for this Standard Ebooks edition."

memes.label: Color Line
memes.description: The social, legal, and psychological boundary between white and Black Americans — the central target of Schuyler's satire in Black No More

memes.label: Index of Concepts
memes.description: Complete index of all concepts in Black No More — thematic, scientific, political, and social ideas explored in Schuyler's satire. Dynamically generated from page metadata.

memes.label: Cosmetic Whitening Industry
memes.description: The pre-existing industries selling skin-lightening creams and hair straighteners to Black consumers, bankrupted by the Black-No-More treatment

memes.label: Crookman's Monograph
memes.description: In the epilogue, Surgeon-General Dr. Junius Crookman publishes a monograph revealing that Black-No-More patients are two to three shades lighter than "old Caucasians," triggering a nationwide social reversal in which whiteness becomes stigmatized

memes.label: Crookman Sanitarium
memes.description: The Harlem medical facility where Dr. Crookman performs the Black-No-More treatment, processing one hundred patients per day and generating enormous profits in the novel's early chapters

memes.label: Democratic Party
memes.description: The political party that absorbs the white supremacist alliance in the novel's presidential campaign; Schuyler's satire of how racial politics serves partisan power.

memes.label: Douglass Bank
memes.description: Black-owned Harlem bank where Bunny Brown worked as a teller; one of several financial institutions swept up in the economic frenzy caused by the Black-No-More treatment.

memes.label: Down-With-White-Prejudice-League
memes.description: The reverse-discrimination advocacy organization founded in the novel's epilogue by Karl von Beerde (formerly Dr. Shakespeare Agamemnon Beard) to defend the rights of the newly-pale minority after Crookman's monograph triggers anti-pale prejudice

memes.label: Dr. Brocker
memes.description: The physician attending Helen Fisher's childbirth — a knowing witness to the treatment's consequences who reveals his own mixed-race ancestry

memes.label: Dr. Junius Crookman
memes.description: The Black scientist who invents Black-No-More, a treatment that transforms Black people into Caucasians — a "Race Man" whose solution to the race problem is to eliminate Blackness, embodying the novel's central satirical contradictions

memes.label: Dr. Yusaburo Noguchi
memes.description: The Japanese scientist mentioned in the Preface whose 1929 claim to be able to "change a Negro into a white man" provides the real-world inspiration for Crookman's fictional treatment

memes.label: Easter Sunday Rally and Wedding (1934)
memes.description: On Easter Sunday 1934, the Knights of Nordica held a massive rally celebrating their first anniversary and one-millionth member, followed by the wedding of Helen Givens and Matthew Fisher

memes.label: Ebervale Sanitarium
memes.description: The second Black-No-More facility, established as the corporation expands beyond Harlem, representing the treatment's geographic spread and the industrialization of racial transformation

memes.label: Edgecombe Avenue
memes.description: The prestigious Harlem address where Black-No-More, Incorporated establishes its corporate offices, representing the pinnacle of Black elite achievement and the imminent dissolution of that elite

memes.label: Egyptienne Stain
memes.description: Skin-darkening concoction invented by Sari Blandine and patented as Blandine's Egyptienne Stain; reverses the racial dynamics of Black-No-More in the novel's satirical epilogue by making darkened skin the new social fashion.

memes.label: Eugenics Thought
memes.description: The pseudoscientific ideology of racial improvement through selective breeding that flourished in early 20th-century America, which the novel parodies by demonstrating that "racial improvement" — becoming white — leads to chaos rather than progress

memes.label: Index of Events
memes.description: Complete index of key plot events in Black No More — turning points, revelations, and episodes with narrative significance. Dynamically generated from page metadata.

memes.label: Founding of Black-No-More, Incorporated
memes.description: Following Max Disher's successful treatment, Dr. Junius Crookman, Hank Johnson, and Chuck Foster formally incorporate Black-No-More, Incorporated and open the first sanitarium in Harlem, launching the commercial enterprise that transforms millions of Black Americans

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "Subtitle page: Being an Account of the Strange and Wonderful Workings of Science in the Land of the Free, AD 1933–1940."

memes.label: George Schuyler
memes.description: The author of Black No More (1931) — a provocative Harlem Renaissance journalist and satirist whose contrarian racial politics shaped the novel's distinctive voice

memes.label: President Harold Goosie
memes.description: Republican President of the United States during the events of the novel — an ineffectual, platitudinous politician who requests Congress to investigate Black-No-More, runs for re-election, and ultimately wins in the chaos following the ancestry revelation

memes.label: Gorman Gay
memes.description: National Chairman of the Republican Party who instructs Attorney General Brybe to dismiss the Negro leaders' resolution against Black-No-More because "there's no money behind it" — a satire of the party machine's reduction of racial justice to campaign finance calculus

memes.label: Hank Johnson
memes.description: The exuberant, persuasive promoter who becomes Dr. Crookman's business partner and the driving force behind Black-No-More, Incorporated's nationwide expansion — the novel's portrait of Black entrepreneurial hustle turned cynical profit machine

memes.label: Happy Hill
memes.description: The Black neighborhood referenced in the novel's final chapter, where the last vestiges of segregated Black community persist, named with the same dark irony as "Paradise"

memes.label: Harlem, New York City
memes.description: The Manhattan neighborhood that was the cultural capital of Black America in the 1920s–1930s — where Max Disher begins his journey, where Dr. Crookman opens his first sanitarium, and where the Black-No-More phenomenon first disrupts the economic ecosystem of Black institutional life

memes.label: Harlem in Black No More
memes.description: Thematic index of people, places, organizations, and events connected to Harlem in George S. Schuyler's Black No More (1931), dynamically generated from pages tagged with "Harlem". Covers the novel's depiction of the Harlem Renaissance's social world — its nightlife, business elite, beauty culture, and political organizations.

memes.label: Harlem Renaissance Satire
memes.description: Schuyler's satirical relationship to the Harlem Renaissance cultural movement, which the novel critiques as an elite aesthetic project disconnected from the material concerns of ordinary Black Americans

memes.label: Helen Fisher
memes.description: Matthew Fisher's wife and Dr. Crookman's daughter — a modern, educated woman caught between her father's scientific ambitions and her husband's political secrets, whose pregnancy and the birth of a dark-skinned son expose the truth about the Black-No-More treatment's impermanence

memes.label: Helen Givens
memes.description: The white Southern belle from Atlanta whose rejection of Max at the Honky Tonk Club triggers his transformation, and whom he later marries as Matthew Fisher

memes.label: Rev. Herbert Gronne
memes.description: Negro leader and president of Dunbar University — a former professor, social worker, and minister who has "received the approval of the white folks" through carefully calibrated rhetoric that sounds radical to Negroes but conservative to white trustees, whose institutional survival instinct is exposed when Black-No-More collapses his student body to sixty-five

memes.label: Honky Tonk Club
memes.description: The Harlem nightclub where the novel opens, a racially mixed social space whose atmosphere of pleasure and possibility is shattered by the racial insult that propels Max Disher toward the Black-No-More treatment

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Publication and copyright information for this Standard Ebooks edition."

memes.label: Jim Crow Aesthetics
memes.description: The visible markers — skin color, hair texture, dress, gesture — through which racial identity was read in Jim Crow America, which the Black-No-More treatment renders unreliable, exposing the performative nature of racial categorization

memes.label: Joseph Bonds
memes.description: Former head of the Negro Data League; a "rat-faced" racial data entrepreneur who transitions from Black advocacy to Republican political campaigning after becoming white.

memes.label: Knights of Nordica
memes.description: The novel's satirical version of the Ku Klux Klan, founded by Reverend Henry Givens as a profitable vehicle for racial fear-mongering that grows into a national political machine and ultimately propels its founder to the presidency

memes.label: Ku Klux Klan
memes.description: The real-world white supremacist terrorist organization that the Knights of Nordica parodies, referenced implicitly throughout the novel as the model for Nordica's structure and rituals

memes.label: Lynching
memes.description: Extralegal racial violence as both social reality and political commodity in Schuyler's satirical treatment of American race relations.

memes.label: Madame Sisseretta Blandish
memes.description: A minor character representing the Black beauty industry — a purveyor of skin-lightening products whose business is destroyed by the Black-No-More treatment

memes.label: Madeline Scranton
memes.description: Woman who telegraphs Bunny Brown in the novel's climax with news of Senator Kretin's lynching, Snobbcraft and Buggerie's flight, and Goosie and Gump's re-election — her telegram is the narrative device through which Schuyler delivers the novel's chaotic resolution in staccato bursts

memes.label: Matthew Fisher
memes.description: Matthew Fisher is the post-treatment identity of Max Disher, the novel's protagonist. After undergoing the Black-No-More treatment, he rises to become Grand Exalted Giraw of the Knights of Nordica and marries Helen Givens.

memes.label: Max Disher / Matthew Fisher
memes.description: The novel's protagonist — a Harlem man about town who becomes the first patient of the Black-No-More treatment and reinvents himself as Matthew Fisher, infiltrating the white supremacist Knights of Nordica

memes.label: Max Disher's Black-No-More Treatment
memes.description: In Chapter 2, Max Disher becomes the first person to undergo Dr. Junius Crookman's Black-No-More treatment at the Harlem sanitarium, emerging as a white man — the inciting event that sets the novel's central plot in motion

memes.label: Media and Propaganda in Black No More
Miss Binks
memes.description: A minor female character appearing in the novel's social scenes — representing the Black women of Harlem whose world is transformed by the treatment

memes.label: Mississippi
memes.description: The Deep South state that serves as a locus for the novel's satire of Southern white supremacy — home to Senator Bosh, site of the novel's references to lynching and racial terror, and the symbolic heartland of the political forces that resist Black-No-More

memes.label: Moronia Broadcasting Company
memes.description: Satirical radio network in Black No More that broadcasts political speeches and racial propaganda; its name ("Moronia" suggesting moron + -ia) reflects Schuyler's mockery of mass media's role in racial politics.

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: Statistical ranking of the most cross-referenced pages in the Black No More wiki. Sorted by total backlink count, this page highlights the most connected entities — characters, concepts, places, and organizations central to Schuyler's narrative.

memes.label: Mrs. Crookman
memes.description: Dr. Crookman's "petite, stylishly-dressed" wife — a "beautiful little octoroon" whose ability to pass for white is remarked upon by Crookman's business partners, and who manages her husband's departure for his European tour in the novel's opening chapters

memes.label: Mrs. Givens
memes.description: Wife of Reverend Henry Givens and mother of Helen Givens — a devout Christian, former Klanswoman, and embodiment of the hypocrisy Schuyler satirizes in Southern white womanhood

memes.label: Mulatto
memes.description: The racial category for persons of mixed Black and white ancestry; in Schuyler's novel, a term deployed to satirize the absurdity of racial classification itself.

memes.label: Dr. Napoleon Wellington Jackson
memes.description: Secretary and chairman of the Committee for the Preservation of Negro Racial Integrity under Dr. Beard — a scholar of dubious credentials who writes indignant letters, composes sorrow songs, argues that plantation shouts surpass Beethoven's symphonies, and reverts to spiritual-singing when it serves his interests

memes.label: National Social Equality League
memes.description: Satirical version of the N.A.A.C.P., a militant civil rights organization whose income and purpose collapse as the Black-No-More treatment eliminates its constituency

memes.label: Negro Data League
memes.description: A racial statistics and research organization led by Joseph Bonds that collects demographic data on Black Americans and collapses when the Black-No-More treatment eliminates its subject population

memes.label: Conference of Negro Leaders (December 1933)
memes.description: On December 1, 1933, the National Social Equality League convened a conference of the country's most prominent Negro leaders at its New York headquarters to formulate a collective response to the Black-No-More sanitariums' mass whitening of the Black population

memes.label: The Negro Leadership Class in Black No More
memes.description: Thematic index of Schuyler's satire of the professional Negro leadership class — those whose careers, institutions, and incomes depend on the perpetuation of racial segregation, and whose response to Black-No-More exposes the economic self-interest beneath the rhetoric of racial advancement

memes.label: Negro Problem
memes.description: The period discourse construct that Schuyler's novel systematically deconstructs — the framing of Black existence as a social difficulty requiring solution.

memes.label: Negro Weekly Newspapers
memes.description: The network of Black newspapers that constitute the novel's chorus of racial commentary, whose advertising base collapses when the whitening industry is destroyed by the Black-No-More treatment

memes.label: New York City
memes.description: The primary Northern setting of the novel, where Harlem is located and where the Black-No-More treatment originates, representing urban modernity against the rural Southern past

memes.label: Nordica Hall
memes.description: The Knights of Nordica's flag-draped meeting hall in Atlanta, where white supremacist rallies are staged and where Max Disher, as Matthew Fisher, rises through the organization's ranks

memes.label: Harlem Numbers Racket
memes.description: The underground lottery that was Harlem's largest illegal economy, where Hank Johnson made his fortune before financing Dr. Crookman's venture

memes.label: One-Drop Rule
memes.description: The legal and social principle that any person with any known African ancestry is classified as Black, which the novel satirizes through the hereditary anxiety caused by the non-hereditary treatment

memes.label: Ancient and Honorable Order of Crocodiles
memes.description: Harlem's largest and most prosperous Black fraternal secret society, of which Hank Johnson was elected Grand Permanent Shogun, representing the Black institutional world dissolved by the treatment

memes.label: Index of Organizations
memes.description: Complete index of all organizations in Black No More — businesses, political parties, hate groups, social clubs, and religious bodies. Dynamically generated from page metadata.

memes.label: Paradise Mill / Blickdoff-Hortzenboff Cotton Mill
memes.description: The cotton mill in Paradise, South Carolina, owned by two German immigrants — a site of labor exploitation where workers earn "around fifteen dollars a week" for an "eleven hours" workday, and the setting for Matthew Fisher's intervention in a threatened strike that reveals how the Knights of Nordica's racial ideology serves capital, not workers

memes.label: Paradise, South Carolina
memes.description: The Southern mill town where the novel's labor politics play out, as whitened Negroes and white workers confront each other in a company-owned dystopia, revealing the economic basis of racial antagonism

memes.label: Paradise, South Carolina
memes.description: The South Carolina factory town where the Blickdoff-Hortzenboff cotton mill operates — a company town that becomes a flashpoint in the Knights of Nordica's labor politics

memes.label: Phyllis Wheatley Hotel
memes.description: The Harlem hotel where Dr. Crookman stays and announces his discovery to the Black press, named after the celebrated 18th-century African American poet

memes.label: Index of Places
memes.description: Complete index of all places with narrative significance in Black No More — cities, neighborhoods, buildings, and regions. Dynamically generated from page metadata.

memes.label: Politics in Black No More
memes.description: Thematic index of political organizations, figures, and concepts in George S. Schuyler's Black No More (1931), dynamically generated from pages tagged with "politics". Covers the novel's satirical treatment of American political parties, racial patronage networks, and the machinery of white supremacy.

memes.label: "Preface"
memes.description: "Preface to Black No More, discussing the historical context of racial transformation."

memes.label: President-Elect Hornbill
memes.description: Goosie's successor, inaugurated after the epilogue's racial reversal — his administration presides over the "mulatto-minded" America where skin-darkening stains have become a national fashion and whiteness itself has become suspect

memes.label: Race and Economics
memes.description: The economic interests that sustain racial hierarchy, which the novel exposes by showing how the Black-No-More treatment disrupts industries, organizations, and political structures dependent on racial division

memes.label: Race and Racial Concepts
memes.description: Thematic index of race-related concepts, characters, and events in George S. Schuyler's Black No More (1931), dynamically generated from pages tagged with "race". Covers the novel's exploration of racial identity, classification, passing, and the social construction of race.

memes.label: Race Man
memes.description: An early-20th-century term for a Black man publicly committed to racial uplift — satirized in Black No More through Dr. Crookman, whose personal life and professional project both contradict his public posture as a "Race Man"

memes.label: Race Science
memes.description: The pseudoscientific theories of racial difference that the novel lampoons, embodied in Dr. Crookman's glandular treatment that transforms Black people into white people

memes.label: Racial Identity
memes.description: The question of what constitutes racial identity — biology, culture, law, or self-identification — that the novel destabilizes through the mass adoption of the Black-No-More treatment

memes.label: Racial Integrity
memes.description: The legal and ideological construct invoked by both white supremacists and Black race leaders in Schuyler's novel; a symmetrical satire of racial essentialism from both directions.

memes.label: Racial Passing
memes.description: The act of light-skinned African Americans presenting as white, which the novel's Black-No-More treatment makes universally available, satirically dissolving the color line

memes.label: Racial Pride
memes.description: The ideology of collective racial identity and uplift among African Americans, which Schuyler satirizes as both a defense against white supremacy and a self-serving posture of the Black elite

memes.label: Republican Party
memes.description: In Black No More, the Republican Party is the incumbent party of President Goosie, entangled with Black-No-More, Inc. financing and struggling against the Democratic Party's white supremacy platform.

memes.label: Harlem Republican Party Machine
memes.description: The Harlem political machine in which Chuck Foster held an "important place," representing the entanglement of Black political organizations with the economic interests the novel satirizes

memes.label: Reverend Henry Givens
memes.description: The cynical founder and Imperial Grand Wizard of the Knights of Nordica, who privately despises the white supremacist ideology he profits from promoting, and ultimately becomes President of the United States

memes.label: Rev. John Whiffle
memes.description: A minister and political power broker in Black No More; described as "a power in the party" during the negotiations over the white supremacist presidential ticket in ch10.

memes.label: Richmond, Virginia
memes.description: Headquarters of the Anglo-Saxon Association of America and the site of Dr. Samuel Buggerie's genealogical research — the city where the novel's climactic revelation about the racial ancestry of America's white elite unfolds

memes.label: Sandol Phillips
memes.description: Dr. Crookman's Senegalese associate — a former French Army aviator whose physical transformation from "very black man" with "bushy Negro hair, flat nose and full lips" to "the most Nordic looking person in the room" serves as the living proof of Black-No-More's efficacy at Crookman's press conference, and who later pilots Crookman's aircraft to Denver

memes.label: Santop Licorice
memes.description: Founder and leader of the Back-to-Africa Society; a comic figure whose racial program is revealed as another form of self-interested exploitation.

memes.label: Sari Blandine
memes.description: Inventor of Blandine's Egyptienne Stain, the skin-darkening product that reverses America's racial hierarchy in the novel's epilogue — formerly the Harlem beauty culturist Madame Sisseretta Blandish

memes.label: Satire in Black No More
memes.description: Thematic index of satirical elements in George S. Schuyler's Black No More (1931), dynamically generated from pages tagged with "satire". Covers the novel's satirical treatment of race, politics, business, and American society.

memes.label: Senator Bosh
memes.description: Mississippi senator who, facing reelection, referenced Crookman's monograph in the Congressional Record to capitalize on the racial anxieties generated by the ancestry revelations.

memes.label: Senator Kretin
memes.description: A Democratic senator who delivers the keynote speech at the party convention; Schuyler's nominal satire of political vacuity (his name means "cretin").

memes.label: Seventh Avenue, Harlem
memes.description: The main thoroughfare of Harlem — the street where Max Disher strolls as one of the neighborhood's "gay blades" before his transformation, representing the vibrant Black social world that Black-No-More will dissolve

memes.label: Dr. Shakespeare Agamemnon Beard
memes.description: Founder and leader of the National Social Equality League — an erudite, pompous Negro leader who writes scholarly denunciations of Caucasians while secretly admiring them, and later re-emerges as "Karl von Beerde" to lead the Down-With-White-Prejudice-League, proving that the racial grievance industry is transferable across color lines

memes.label: Simeon Dump
memes.description: Director of the Anglo-Saxon Association of America in Black No More; a key figure in the political maneuvering of ch10 who helps fund the Knights of Nordica's electoral campaign.

memes.label: Walter Snickelfritz
memes.description: A minor character representing the white working-class recruits of the Knights of Nordica — a man whose economic grievances are channeled into racial resentment

memes.label: Social Equality
memes.description: Central satirical concept in Black No More representing the stated goal of Negro civil rights organizations, which the novel treats as fundamentally self-interested and subject to economic incentives.

memes.label: Social Mortality
memes.description: The collapse of Black social institutions — churches, newspapers, fraternal orders, businesses — as the Black population vanishes into whiteness, a satirical exploration of what is lost when the oppressed cease to exist as a distinct group

memes.label: Southern Democratic Party Machine
memes.description: The political apparatus of the one-party Jim Crow South, whose electoral strategies collapse when the Black-No-More treatment eliminates the visible Black population they had demonized

memes.label: Standard Ebooks
memes.description: The volunteer-driven project that produced this ebook edition of Black No More, mentioned in the imprint as the publisher of the source text

memes.label: The Scimitar
memes.description: A New York City tabloid newspaper that purchases and publishes Max Disher's exclusive story after his Black-No-More treatment, representing the sensationalist press in Schuyler's satire of media and race

memes.label: The Southland
memes.description: The American South as depicted in Black No More — less a geographic region than a political and ideological landscape where the racial order Schuyler satirizes is most deeply entrenched

memes.label: The Warning
memes.description: Propaganda newspaper published by the Knights of Nordica, edited by Matthew Fisher, used to stoke white racial fear and mobilize political support.

memes.label: Times Square, New York City
memes.description: The commercial heart of New York City — site of the Black-No-More advertising blitz, the Down-With-White-Prejudice-League's offices, and a recurring setting in the novel's map of racialized urban space

memes.label: Times Square District
memes.description: Manhattan commercial district where Black-No-More, Inc., the National Social Equality League, and other race-related enterprises establish their headquarters in the novel; symbolic center of the racial-capitalist transformation.

memes.label: "Title Page"
memes.description: "Title page of Black No More by George Schuyler."

memes.label: True Faith Christ Lovers' Church
memes.description: Fundamentalist Christian sect headquartered in Happy Hill, Mississippi, led by Rev. Alex McPhule; the novel's satirical depiction of Southern white evangelicalism and its complicity in racial violence.

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Uncopyright notice dedicating this ebook to the public domain."

memes.label: Vanishing Mammy
memes.description: Popular minstrel song performed at the Knights of Nordica Easter Sunday rally, exemplifying the novel's satire of white nostalgia for the "old Negro" of plantation mythology.

relations.label: Knights of Nordica Easter Sunday rally

memes.label: Vitiligo
memes.description: The skin disease whose mechanism Dr. Crookman artificially replicates to create the Black-No-More treatment; the novel's scientific premise and its ultimate ironic reversal.

memes.label: Walter Brybe
memes.description: Attorney General of the United States who earned his position "through long and faithful service helping large corporations to circumvent the federal laws" — dismisses the Negro leaders' resolution after consulting with Gorman Gay, the Republican National Chairman, on the grounds that political expediency trumps racial justice

memes.label: Walter Williams
memes.description: A Republican campaigner who "had passed for a Negro for years on the strength of a part-Negro grandparent" before returning to whiteness; a minor figure illustrating the arbitrariness of racial classification.

memes.label: Washington, DC
memes.description: The seat of federal political power in *Black No More* — where Attorney General Walter Brybe coordinates the government's response, legislation is shaped by bribery, and the White House fields truckloads of letters condemning Black-No-More, Incorporated

memes.label: Wheatley Trust Company
memes.description: Harlem financial institution named for the poet Phyllis Wheatley; one of several banks and trust companies overwhelmed by depositor panic during the Black-No-More exodus.

memes.label: White Citizens Councils
memes.description: The Southern white citizens' groups that emerge in response to the Black-No-More treatment, pressuring politicians to legislate against racial passing

memes.label: White Supremacy
memes.description: The ideology of white racial superiority that the novel lampoons as a system of economic self-interest masquerading as natural law — exposed as a confidence game when the Black-No-More treatment makes whiteness purchasable for fifty dollars

memes.label: White Supremacy in Black No More
memes.description: Thematic index of organizations, individuals, and concepts related to white supremacy in George S. Schuyler's Black No More (1931), dynamically generated from pages tagged with "white supremacy". Covers the novel's satirical anatomy of organized racism — the Knights of Nordica, the Ku Klux Klan, the Anglo-Saxon Association, and the ideology of racial purity that the Black-No-More treatment ultimately undermines.

memes.label: Whitening Industry
memes.description: The pre-existing market for skin-lightening creams and hair-straightening products that the Black-No-More treatment renders obsolete — Schuyler's satirical entry point into the economics of racial self-modification
